records.ety: [L. injuriari, fr. injuria injury, perh. through F. injurier to insult, in OF. also, to injure; or perhaps fr. E. injury, or F. injure injury. See Injury.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Injured (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Injuring.]

defs: To do harm to; to impair the excellence and value of; to hurt; to damage;
To hurt or wound, as the person; to impair soundness, as of health.
To damage or lessen the value of, as goods or estate.
To slander, tarnish, or impair, as reputation or character.
To impair or diminish, as happiness or virtue.
To give pain to, as the sensibilities or the feelings; to grieve; to annoy.
To impair, as the intellect or mind.
quotes: When have I injured thee? when done thee wrong?
